429	    def self_check_during_idle(self) -> None:
430	        self.new_token_ratio = self.init_new_token_ratio
431	        idle_sleeper = self.__dict__.get("idle_sleeper")
432	        if idle_sleeper is not None:
433	            idle_sleeper.maybe_sleep()
434	
435	    def self_check_during_busy(self) -> None:
436	        return None
437	
438	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
439	    # Composition: delegate missing attributes to the upstream class
440	    # ------------------------------------------------------------------
441	
442	    def __getattr__(self, name: str):
443	        """Look up methods on the upstream SGLang Scheduler class.
444	
445	        This gives us access to the full scheduling MRO (batch selection,
446	        result processing, memory checks, etc.) without inheriting.
447	        """
448	        if name == "grammar_queue":
449	            value = []
450	            self.__dict__[name] = value
451	            return value
452	        if name == "grammar_backend":
453	            self.__dict__[name] = None
454	            return None
455	
456	        try:
457	            attr = getattr(_Upstream, name)
458	        except AttributeError:
459	            raise AttributeError(
460	                f"'{type(self).__name__}' has no attribute {name!r}"
461	            ) from None
462	
463	        # Bind unbound methods to this instance so they use our state
464	        if callable(attr):
465	            return types.MethodType(attr, self)
466	        return attr
467	
468	    def _init_parallel_state(self, tp_worker: Any) -> None:
469	        enable_dp_attention = self.server_args.enable_dp_attention
470	        self.attn_tp_rank, self.attn_tp_size, self.attn_dp_rank = (
471	            compute_dp_attention_world_info(
472	                enable_dp_attention,
473	                self.tp_rank,
474	                self.tp_size,
475	                self.dp_size,
476	            )
477	        )
478	
479	        self.tp_group = tp_worker.get_tp_group()
480	        self.tp_cpu_group = self.tp_group.cpu_group
481	        self.attn_tp_group = tp_worker.get_attention_tp_group()
482	        self.attn_tp_cpu_group = tp_worker.get_attention_tp_cpu_group()
483	
484	        if enable_dp_attention:
485	            self.cpu_group = self.attn_tp_cpu_group
486	            self.entry_rank = self.attn_tp_group.first_rank
487	            self.is_entry_rank = self.attn_tp_rank == 0
488	        else:
489	            self.cpu_group = self.tp_cpu_group
490	            self.entry_rank = self.tp_group.first_rank
491	            self.is_entry_rank = self.tp_group.rank_in_group == 0
492	
493	        self.pad_input_ids_func = tp_worker.get_pad_input_ids_func()
494	
495	        self.current_scheduler_metrics_enabled = (
496	            self.attn_tp_rank == 0 or self.enable_metrics_for_all_schedulers
497	        )
498	
499	    def recv_requests(self):
500	        """Drain inbox on rank 0 and broadcast scheduler inputs to TP followers."""
501	        recv_msgs = self._recv_scheduler_messages()
502	        new_reqs: list = []
503	        for msg in recv_msgs:
504	            if msg.request_id in self._aborted_request_ids:
505	                continue
506	
507	            if msg.type == "new_request":
508	                new_reqs.append(msg.data)
509	            elif msg.type == "stream_chunk":
510	                self._on_stream_chunk(msg.request_id, msg.data)
511	            elif msg.type == "stream_done":
512	                self._on_stream_done(msg.request_id)
513	
514	        return new_reqs
515	
516	    def _recv_scheduler_messages(self) -> list[IncomingMessage]:
517	        if self.tp_size == 1:
518	            return self._drain_local_inbox()
519	
520	        recv_msgs = self._drain_local_inbox() if self.is_entry_rank else []
521	        return broadcast_pyobj(
522	            recv_msgs,
523	            self.tp_group.rank,
524	            self.tp_cpu_group,
525	            src=self.tp_group.ranks[0],
526	        )
527	
528	    def _drain_local_inbox(self) -> list[IncomingMessage]:
529	        recv_msgs: list[IncomingMessage] = []
530	        while True:
531	            try:
532	                recv_msgs.append(self.inbox.get_nowait())
533	            except _queue_mod.Empty:
534	                break
535	        return recv_msgs
536	
537	    def process_input_requests(self, recv_reqs):
538	        """Convert incoming payloads to SGLang Reqs and enqueue."""
539	        self._drain_request_build_results()
540	        recv_reqs, rejected_reqs = self._stage_request_build_payloads(recv_reqs)
541	        for payload in rejected_reqs:
542	            self._reject_request_build_backlog_overflow(payload)
543	        for payload in recv_reqs:
544	            req_id = payload.request_id
545	            with self._request_admission_lock:
546	                if (
547	                    req_id in self._aborted_request_ids
548	                    or req_id in self._pending_request_builds
549	                ):
550	                    continue
551	            buffered_chunks = self._pending_stream_chunks.pop(req_id, [])
552	            existing_chunks = list(getattr(payload, "prefetched_chunks", []) or [])
553	            if existing_chunks:
554	                existing_chunks.extend(buffered_chunks)
555	                payload.prefetched_chunks = existing_chunks
556	            else:
557	                payload.prefetched_chunks = buffered_chunks
558	            pending_stream_done = req_id in self._pending_stream_done
559	            payload.prefetched_stream_done = pending_stream_done
560	            if not self._is_request_build_ready(
561	                payload,
562	                pending_stream_done=pending_stream_done,
563	            ):
564	                self._deferred_request_payloads[req_id] = payload
565	                continue
566	            active_stage = _get_active_stage()
567	            request_build_executor = self._request_build_executor
568	            if request_build_executor is not None:
569	                with self._request_admission_lock:
570	                    if (
571	                        req_id in self._aborted_request_ids
572	                        or req_id in self._pending_request_builds
573	                    ):
574	                        continue
575	                    future = request_build_executor.submit(
576	                        self._run_request_builder, payload, active_stage
577	                    )
578	                    self._pending_request_builds[req_id] = (
579	                        payload,
580	                        pending_stream_done,
581	                        future,
582	                    )
583	                    self._request_build_max_pending_observed = max(
584	                        self._request_build_max_pending_observed,
585	                        len(self._pending_request_builds),
586	                    )
587	                continue
588	            try:
589	                req_data = self._run_request_builder(payload, active_stage)
590	            except Exception as exc:
591	                logger.exception(f"OmniScheduler: request builder failed for {req_id}")
592	                self._emit_request_error(req_id, exc)
593	                self.abort(req_id)
594	                continue
595	            self._enqueue_built_request(payload, pending_stream_done, req_data)
596	        self._drain_request_build_results()
597	
598	    def _run_request_builder(self, payload: Any, active_stage: str | None) -> Any:
599	        req_id = payload.request_id
600	        _emit_event(
601	            request_id=req_id,
602	            stage=active_stage,
603	            event_name="scheduler_request_build_start",
604	        )
605	        req_data = self._request_builder(payload)
606	        _emit_event(
607	            request_id=req_id,
608	            stage=active_stage,
609	            event_name="scheduler_request_build_end",
610	        )
611	        return req_data
612	
613	    def _stage_request_build_payloads(
614	        self, recv_reqs: list[Any]
615	    ) -> tuple[list[Any], list[Any]]:
616	        if self._request_build_executor is None:
617	            return list(recv_reqs), []
618	
619	        with self._request_admission_lock:
620	            backlog = self._backlogged_request_build_payloads
621	            pending_builds = self._pending_request_builds
622	            rejected: list[Any] = []
623	            backlog_ids = {payload.request_id for payload in backlog}
624	            capacity = max(
625	                0,
626	                self.request_build_max_pending - len(pending_builds),
627	            )
628	            selected: list[Any] = []
629	            selected_ids: set[str] = set()
630	            while capacity > 0 and backlog:
631	                payload = backlog.popleft()
632	                req_id = payload.request_id
633	                backlog_ids.discard(req_id)
634	                if req_id in self._aborted_request_ids or req_id in pending_builds:
635	                    continue
636	                selected.append(payload)
637	                selected_ids.add(req_id)
638	                capacity -= 1
639	
640	            for payload in recv_reqs:
641	                req_id = payload.request_id
642	                if (
643	                    req_id in self._aborted_request_ids
644	                    or req_id in pending_builds
645	                    or req_id in backlog_ids
646	                    or req_id in selected_ids
647	                ):
648	                    continue
649	                if capacity > 0:
650	                    selected.append(payload)
651	                    selected_ids.add(req_id)
652	                    capacity -= 1
653	                    continue
654	                if len(backlog) >= self._request_build_backlog_limit:
655	                    rejected.append(payload)
656	                    continue
657	                backlog.append(payload)
658	                backlog_ids.add(req_id)
659	            return selected, rejected
660	
661	    def _reject_request_build_backlog_overflow(self, payload: Any) -> None:
662	        req_id = payload.request_id
663	        error = RuntimeError(
664	            "request-build backlog is full "
665	            f"(backlog_limit={self._request_build_backlog_limit})"
666	        )
667	        logger.warning("Rejecting request %s before build: %s", req_id, error)
668	        self._emit_request_error(req_id, error)
669	        self.abort(req_id)
670	
671	    def _drain_request_build_results(self) -> None:
672	        while True:
673	            with self._request_admission_lock:
674	                if not self._pending_request_builds:
675	                    return
676	                req_id, (payload, pending_stream_done, future) = next(
677	                    iter(self._pending_request_builds.items())
678	                )
679	                if not future.done():
680	                    return
681	                self._pending_request_builds.pop(req_id, None)
682	                if req_id in self._aborted_request_ids:
683	                    continue
684	            try:
685	                req_data = future.result()
686	            except Exception as exc:
687	                with self._request_admission_lock:
688	                    if req_id in self._aborted_request_ids:
689	                        continue
690	                logger.exception(f"OmniScheduler: request builder failed for {req_id}")
691	                self._emit_request_error(req_id, exc)
692	                self.abort(req_id)
693	                continue
694	            with self._request_admission_lock:
695	                if req_id in self._aborted_request_ids:
696	                    continue
697	                self._enqueue_built_request(
698	                    payload,
699	                    pending_stream_done,
700	                    req_data,
701	                    request_admission_lock_held=True,
702	                )
703	
704	    def _enqueue_built_request(
705	        self,
706	        payload: Any,
707	        pending_stream_done: bool,
708	        req_data: Any,
709	        *,
710	        request_admission_lock_held: bool = False,
711	    ) -> None:
712	        req_id = payload.request_id
713	        if pending_stream_done:
714	            self._pending_stream_done.discard(req_id)
715	        self._deferred_request_payloads.pop(req_id, None)
716	        req = req_data.req
717	        req._omni_data = req_data
718	        req_id = req.rid
719	        if bool(getattr(req_data, "enforce_request_limits", False)):
720	            error_msg = self._prepare_request_limits(req_data)
721	            if error_msg:
722	                self._emit_request_error(req_id, ValueError(error_msg))
723	                self.abort(req_id)
724	                return
725	        kv_error = self._request_kv_capacity_error(req)
726	        if kv_error is not None:
727	            logger.warning(f"Rejecting request {req_id} before scheduling: {kv_error}")
728	            self._emit_request_error(req_id, ValueError(kv_error))
729	            self.abort(req_id)
730	            return
731	        self._initialize_request_stream_state(req_data, payload)
732	        for chunk in self._pending_stream_chunks.pop(req_id, []) or []:
733	            self._append_stream_chunk(req_data, chunk)
734	        if req_id in self._pending_stream_done:
735	            self._pending_stream_done.discard(req_id)
736	            self._mark_stream_done(req_data)
737	
738	        def enqueue_if_live() -> None:
739	            if req_id in self._aborted_request_ids:
740	                return
741	            _emit_event(
742	                request_id=req_id,
743	                stage=None,
744	                event_name="scheduler_queue_enter",
745	            )
746	            if not hasattr(req, "_coalesce_enqueue_t"):
747	                req._coalesce_enqueue_t = time.perf_counter()
748	            self.waiting_queue.append(req)
749	
750	        if request_admission_lock_held:
751	            enqueue_if_live()
752	        else:
753	            with self._request_admission_lock:
754	                enqueue_if_live()
755	
756	    def _prepare_request_limits(self, req_data: Any) -> str | None:
757	        req = req_data.req
758	        self.init_req_max_new_tokens(req)
759	        error_msg = validate_input_length(
760	            req,
761	            self.max_req_input_len,
762	            allow_auto_truncate=False,
763	        )
764	        if error_msg:
765	            return error_msg
766	        if hasattr(req_data, "max_new_tokens"):
767	            req_data.max_new_tokens = int(req.sampling_params.max_new_tokens)
768	        return None
769	
770	    def _take_deferred_request_payloads(self) -> list[Any]:
771	        if not self._dirty_deferred_request_ids:
772	            return []
773	        deferred: list[Any] = []
774	        for req_id in list(self._dirty_deferred_request_ids):
775	            payload = self._deferred_request_payloads.pop(req_id, None)
776	            if payload is not None:
777	                deferred.append(payload)
778	        self._dirty_deferred_request_ids.clear()
779	        return deferred
780	
781	    def _should_recheck_deferred_request_on_stream_chunk(
782	        self, request_id: str, chunk: Any
783	    ) -> bool:
784	        del request_id, chunk
785	        return True
786	
787	    def _is_request_build_ready(
788	        self,
789	        payload: Any,
790	        *,
791	        pending_stream_done: bool,
792	    ) -> bool:
793	        del payload, pending_stream_done
794	        return True
795	
796	    def _initialize_request_stream_state(self, req_data: Any, payload: Any) -> None:
797	        for chunk in getattr(payload, "prefetched_chunks", []) or []:
798	            self._append_stream_chunk(req_data, chunk)
799	        if bool(getattr(payload, "prefetched_stream_done", False)):
800	            self._mark_stream_done(req_data)
801	
802	    def _request_kv_capacity_error(self, req: Any) -> str | None:
803	        input_len = len(req.origin_input_ids)
804	        max_new_tokens = int(req.sampling_params.max_new_tokens or 0)
805	        required_tokens = input_len + max_new_tokens
806	        kv_capacity = int(self.max_req_len)
807	        if required_tokens <= kv_capacity:
808	            return None
809	
810	        mem_fraction = self.server_args.mem_fraction_static
811	        if mem_fraction is not None:
812	            mem_hint = (
813	                f" Current mem_fraction_static is {mem_fraction:.3f}; try setting "
814	                "--thinker-mem-fraction-static higher."
815	            )
816	        else:
817	            mem_hint = " Try setting a higher --thinker-mem-fraction-static value."
818	
819	        return (
820	            "Request requires more tokens than the thinker KV cache can hold "
821	            f"(input_tokens={input_len}, max_new_tokens={max_new_tokens}, "
822	            f"required_tokens={required_tokens}, kv_capacity={kv_capacity})."
823	            f"{mem_hint}"
824	        )
825	
826	    def _emit_request_error(self, request_id: str, error: Exception) -> None:
827	        if not getattr(self, "is_entry_rank", True):
828	            return
829	        self.outbox.put(
830	            OutgoingMessage(
831	                request_id=request_id,
832	                type="error",
833	                data=error,
834	            )
835	        )
836	
837	    def get_new_batch_prefill(self):
838	        # Note: (maydomine) batch prefill admissions to amortize the fixed step
839	        # cost; the oldest-request deadline survives partial admission and aborts.
840	        if self.prefill_coalesce_requests <= 1 or self.chunked_req is not None:
841	            return _Upstream.get_new_batch_prefill(self)
842	        if self.running_batch is None or self.running_batch.is_empty():
843	            return _Upstream.get_new_batch_prefill(self)
844	        waiting = self.waiting_queue
845	        if not waiting or len(waiting) >= self.prefill_coalesce_requests:
846	            return _Upstream.get_new_batch_prefill(self)
847	        now = time.perf_counter()
848	        oldest = now
849	        for req in waiting:
850	            t = getattr(req, "_coalesce_enqueue_t", None)
851	            if t is None:
852	                t = req._coalesce_enqueue_t = now
853	            oldest = min(oldest, t)
854	        if now - oldest >= self.prefill_coalesce_wait_s:
855	            return _Upstream.get_new_batch_prefill(self)
856	        return None
857	
858	    def run_batch(self, batch, pp_proxy_tensors=None):
859	        try:
860	            return self._run_batch(batch, pp_proxy_tensors)
861	        except Exception as exc:
862	            self._handle_batch_failure(batch, exc)
863	            return _FAILED_BATCH_RESULT
864	
865	    def _run_batch(self, batch, pp_proxy_tensors=None):
866	        """Run a batch through the model runner.
867	
868	        The custom model runner (for example ThinkerModelRunner or a
869	        model-specific talker runner)
870	        accepts a ``SchedulerOutput`` wrapper and returns a
871	        ``ModelRunnerOutput``.  The upstream ``process_batch_result`` expects
872	        a ``GenerationBatchResult``.  We bridge the two formats here.
873	        """
874	        self._emit_prefill_start_for_batch(batch)
875	        if self._model_runner is not None:
876	            # Mirror upstream run_batch's per-forward counter: OmniScheduler
877	            # overrides run_batch, so without this forward_ct stays 0 and
878	            # SGLANG_TEST_RETRACT fires every step. Only the custom-runner path
879	            # needs it (the fallback reaches upstream run_batch, which counts).
880	            self.forward_ct = getattr(self, "forward_ct", 0) + 1
881	            sched_output = self._build_sched_output(batch)
882	            mr_output = self._model_runner.execute(sched_output)
883	            self._emit_stream_output(sched_output, mr_output)
884	            return self._make_batch_result(batch, mr_output)
885	        # Fallback: call upstream's run_batch (uses tp_worker directly)
886	        return _Upstream.run_batch(self, batch, pp_proxy_tensors)
887	
888	    def _build_sched_output(self, batch):
889	        """Wrap a ScheduleBatch into the SchedulerOutput the model runner
890	        expects. Shared by the sync and async (launch) paths."""
891	        from sglang_omni.scheduling.types import SchedulerOutput, SchedulerRequest
892	
893	        sched_reqs = [
894	            SchedulerRequest(request_id=req.rid, data=req._omni_data)
895	            for req in batch.reqs
896	        ]
897	        return SchedulerOutput(requests=sched_reqs, batch_data=batch)
898	
899	    def _emit_stream_output(self, sched_output, mr_output, skip_rids=()) -> None:
900	        """Emit per-request stream chunks from a ModelRunnerOutput. Shared by
901	        the sync and async (resolve) paths. ``skip_rids`` suppresses emission
902	        for requests already finished in an earlier step (the lookahead
903	        overrun) — emitting their extra chunk would corrupt the downstream
904	        vocoder's delayed-code stream. Aborted requests are suppressed for the
905	        same reason: an abort landing mid-step must not ship one more chunk."""
906	        if self._stream_output_builder is None:
907	            return
908	        for sched_req in sched_output.requests:
909	            rid = sched_req.request_id
910	            if rid in skip_rids or rid in self._aborted_request_ids:
911	                continue
912	            req_output = mr_output.outputs[rid]
913	            emitted_any = False
914	            for msg in self._stream_output_builder(rid, sched_req.data, req_output):
915	                if not emitted_any:
916	                    if rid not in self._first_emit_done:
917	                        self._first_emit_done.add(rid)
918	                        _emit_event(
919	                            request_id=rid,
920	                            stage=None,
921	                            event_name="scheduler_first_emit",
922	                        )
923	                    emitted_any = True
924	                self.outbox.put(msg)
925	
926	    @staticmethod
927	    def _make_batch_result(batch, mr_output):
928	        # process_batch_result reads .next_token_ids / .logits_output; the
929	        # model runner already set batch.output_ids during execute/resolve.
930	        from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler import GenerationBatchResult
931	
932	        next_token_ids = batch.output_ids
933	        if isinstance(next_token_ids, torch.Tensor):
934	            batch.input_ids = next_token_ids.to(torch.int64)
935	        return GenerationBatchResult(
936	            logits_output=None,
937	            next_token_ids=next_token_ids,
938	            can_run_cuda_graph=mr_output.can_run_cuda_graph,
939	        )
940	
941	    def _run_batch_launch(self, batch):
942	        """Async: build SchedulerOutput and launch the decode step on the GPU
943	        (forward + sample, then ``post_decode_launch`` publishes the resolve
944	        payload), without waiting. Returns ``(sched_output, pending_step)``; the
945	        caller holds the pending step (launch-first keeps two steps in flight)."""
946	        self._emit_prefill_start_for_batch(batch)
947	        # One forward per launch; mirror upstream run_batch's per-forward
948	        # counter (the matching resolve does no forward, so it must not count).
949	        self.forward_ct = getattr(self, "forward_ct", 0) + 1
950	        sched_output = self._build_sched_output(batch)
951	        pending_step = self._model_runner.execute_launch(sched_output)
952	        return sched_output, pending_step
953	
954	    def _run_batch_resolve(self, batch, sched_output, pending_step, skip_rids=()):
955	        """Async: resolve the given launched step (wait event, host collect),
956	        emit its stream chunks (except overrun reqs in ``skip_rids``), and
957	        return its GenerationBatchResult.
958	
959	        next_token_ids comes from the resolved step's own batch_result, not
960	        ``batch.output_ids`` — the running batch's output_ids was already
961	        consumed (reset to None) by the next step's prepare_for_decode.
962	        """
963	        from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler import GenerationBatchResult
964	
965	        mr_output = self._model_runner.execute_resolve(pending_step)
966	        if mr_output is None:
967	            return _FAILED_BATCH_RESULT
968	        self._emit_stream_output(sched_output, mr_output, skip_rids=skip_rids)
969	        return GenerationBatchResult(
970	            logits_output=None,
971	            next_token_ids=pending_step.batch_result.next_token_ids,
972	            can_run_cuda_graph=mr_output.can_run_cuda_graph,
973	        )
974	
975	    def _handle_batch_failure(self, batch: Any, error: Exception) -> None:
976	        reqs = list(batch.reqs)
977	        request_ids = [req.rid for req in reqs]
978	        logger.exception("OmniScheduler batch failed for requests=%s", request_ids)
979	        for req in reqs:
980	            self._emit_request_error(req.rid, error)
981	            self.abort(req.rid, defer_running_cleanup=False)
982	
983	    def _emit_prefill_start_for_batch(self, batch: ScheduleBatch) -> None:
984	        """Emit once when a request's first executable batch is selected."""
985	        metadata = {
986	            "is_prefill_only": bool(batch.is_prefill_only),
987	            "is_extend_in_batch": bool(batch.is_extend_in_batch),
988	        }
989	        for req in batch.reqs:
990	            rid = req.rid
991	            if rid in self._prefill_start_done:
992	                continue
993	            self._prefill_start_done.add(rid)
994	            _emit_event(
995	                request_id=rid,
996	                stage=None,
997	                event_name="scheduler_prefill_start",
998	                metadata=metadata,
999	            )
1000	
1001	    def stream_output(self, reqs, return_logprob=False, skip_req=None):
1002	        """Intercept finished requests and emit to outbox.
1003	
1004	        Upstream calls this after process_batch_result to send results
1005	        to the detokenizer via ZMQ.  We capture finished requests here
1006	        and put them in the outbox so Stage can route them downstream.
1007	        """
1008	        for req in reqs:
1009	            if skip_req is not None and req is skip_req:
1010	                continue
1011	            if not req.finished():
1012	                continue
1013	
1014	            rid = req.rid
1015	            if rid in self._aborted_request_ids:
1016	                # note (Gaokai): an abort landing mid-step finishes here via
1017	                # FINISH_ABORT; run the cleanup abort() deferred (callbacks are
1018	                # idempotent) and drop the stale terminal result so it cannot
1019	                # resurrect the request downstream.
1020	                if self._abort_callback is not None:
1021	                    try:
1022	                        self._abort_callback(rid)
1023	                    except Exception:
1024	                        logger.exception(
1025	                            "OmniScheduler: abort cleanup failed for %s", rid
1026	                        )
1027	                self._first_emit_done.discard(rid)
1028	                self._prefill_start_done.discard(rid)
1029	                continue
1030	
1031	            # Build result payload from the Req
1032	            data = req._omni_data
1033	            data.output_ids = list(req.output_ids)
1034	            data.weight_version = self.server_args.weight_version
1035	            finished_reason = req.finished_reason
1036	            data.finish_reason = (
1037	                finished_reason.to_json().get("type")
1038	                if finished_reason is not None
1039	                else None
1040	            )
1041	            try:
1042	                result = self._result_adapter(data)
1043	            except Exception as exc:
1044	                logger.exception(
1045	                    "OmniScheduler result adapter failed for request %s", rid
1046	                )
1047	                self._first_emit_done.discard(rid)
1048	                self._prefill_start_done.discard(rid)
1049	                self._emit_request_error(rid, exc)
1050	                continue
1051	            finally:
1052	                data.prefill_input_embeds = None
1053	                data.decode_input_embeds = None
1054	
1055	            self._first_emit_done.discard(rid)
1056	            self._prefill_start_done.discard(rid)
1057	            self.outbox.put(
1058	                OutgoingMessage(
1059	                    request_id=rid,
1060	                    type="result",
1061	                    data=result,
1062	                )
1063	            )
1064	
1065	    def send_to_tokenizer(self):
1066	        """No-op — results are routed through the stage outbox."""
1067	        return None
1068	
1069	    def _on_stream_chunk(self, request_id: str, chunk: Any) -> None:
1070	        req_data = self._find_request_data(request_id)
1071	        if req_data is not None:
1072	            self._append_stream_chunk(req_data, chunk)
1073	            return
1074	        self._pending_stream_chunks.setdefault(request_id, []).append(chunk)
1075	        if (
1076	            request_id in self._deferred_request_payloads
1077	            and self._should_recheck_deferred_request_on_stream_chunk(request_id, chunk)
1078	        ):
1079	            self._dirty_deferred_request_ids.add(request_id)
1080	
1081	    def _on_stream_done(self, request_id: str) -> None:
1082	        req_data = self._find_request_data(request_id)
1083	        if req_data is not None:
1084	            self._mark_stream_done(req_data)
1085	            return
1086	        self._pending_stream_done.add(request_id)
1087	        if request_id in self._deferred_request_payloads:
1088	            self._dirty_deferred_request_ids.add(request_id)
1089	
1090	    def start(self) -> None:
1091	        self._scheduler_thread_id = threading.get_ident()
1092	        self._running = True
1093	        try:
1094	            if getattr(self, "enable_async_decode", False):
1095	                self._event_loop_async_decode()
1096	            elif self.enable_overlap:
1097	                self._event_loop_overlap()
1098	            else:
1099	                self._event_loop_normal()
1100	        finally:
1101	            self._scheduler_thread_id = None
1102	            try:
1103	                self._shutdown_request_build_executor()
1104	            finally:
1105	                self._shutdown_resources()
1106	
1107	    def event_loop(self) -> None:
1108	        self.start()
1109	
1110	    def stop(self) -> None:
1111	        self._running = False
1112	        self._shutdown_resources()
1113	
1114	    def _shutdown_resources(self) -> None:
1115	        with self._shutdown_lock:
1116	            callback = self._shutdown_callback
1117	            self._shutdown_callback = None
1118	        if callback is not None:
1119	            callback()
1120	
1121	    def _shutdown_request_build_executor(self) -> None:
1122	        executor = self._request_build_executor
1123	        if executor is None:
1124	            return
1125	        executor.shutdown(wait=False, cancel_futures=True)
1126	        self._request_build_executor = None
1127	
1128	    def abort(self, request_id: str, *, defer_running_cleanup: bool = True) -> None:
1129	        running_abort = (
1130	            self._mark_running_request_aborted(request_id)
1131	            if defer_running_cleanup
1132	            else False
1133	        )
1134	        with self._request_admission_lock:
1135	            if request_id not in self._aborted_request_ids:
1136	                if len(self._aborted_request_ids) >= _ABORTED_REQUEST_ID_LIMIT:
1137	                    # note (Gaokai): evict oldest-first so a still-quiescing
1138	                    # abort survives.
1139	                    while (
1140	                        len(self._aborted_request_ids) >= _ABORTED_REQUEST_ID_RETAINED
1141	                    ):
1142	                        self._aborted_request_ids.discard(
1143	                            self._aborted_request_id_order.popleft()
1144	                        )
1145	                self._aborted_request_ids.add(request_id)
1146	                self._aborted_request_id_order.append(request_id)
1147	            pending = self._pending_request_builds.pop(request_id, None)
1148	            if pending is not None:
1149	                pending[2].cancel()
1150	            if self._backlogged_request_build_payloads:
1151	                retained = [
1152	                    payload
1153	                    for payload in self._backlogged_request_build_payloads
1154	                    if payload.request_id != request_id
1155	                ]
1156	                self._backlogged_request_build_payloads.clear()
1157	                self._backlogged_request_build_payloads.extend(retained)
1158	            self.waiting_queue = [
1159	                req for req in self.waiting_queue if req.rid != request_id
1160	            ]
1161	        if self._abort_callback is not None and not running_abort:
1162	            try:
1163	                self._abort_callback(request_id)
1164	            except Exception:
1165	                logger.exception(
1166	                    "OmniScheduler: abort cleanup failed for %s", request_id
1167	                )
1168	        self._pending_stream_chunks.pop(request_id, None)
1169	        self._pending_stream_done.discard(request_id)
1170	        self._deferred_request_payloads.pop(request_id, None)
1171	        self._dirty_deferred_request_ids.discard(request_id)
1172	        self._first_emit_done.discard(request_id)
1173	        self._prefill_start_done.discard(request_id)
1174	        if not running_abort:
1175	            self._release_immediate_request_resources(request_id)
1176	            _remove_from_batch(self.running_batch, request_id)
1177	            _remove_from_batch(self.cur_batch, request_id)
1178	            _remove_from_batch(self.last_batch, request_id)
1179	            _remove_from_batch(self._async_pending_batch(), request_id)
1180	        self._drain_inbox_for_request(request_id)

1182	    def admin(
1183	        self, action: str, payload: dict[str, Any] | None = None
1184	    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
1185	        payload = dict(payload or {})
1186	        if self._should_enqueue_admin():
1187	            return self._enqueue_admin(action, payload)
1188	        return self._run_admin_action(action, payload)
1189	
1190	    def _should_enqueue_admin(self) -> bool:
1191	        scheduler_thread_id = getattr(self, "_scheduler_thread_id", None)
1192	        return (
1193	            bool(getattr(self, "_running", False))
1194	            and scheduler_thread_id is not None
1195	            and threading.get_ident() != scheduler_thread_id
1196	        )
1197	
1198	    def _enqueue_admin(self, action: str, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
1199	        timeout_s = float(payload.get("_admin_timeout_s", 300.0))
1200	        queued_payload = dict(payload)
1201	        queued_payload.pop("_admin_timeout_s", None)
1202	        response_queue = _queue_mod.Queue(maxsize=1)
1203	        self._admin_queue.put((action, queued_payload, response_queue))
1204	        try:
1205	            return response_queue.get(timeout=timeout_s)
1206	        except _queue_mod.Empty:
1207	            return {
1208	                "success": False,
1209	                "message": f"admin operation timed out after {timeout_s:.1f}s",
1210	                "error": "admin operation timed out",
1211	            }
1212	
1213	    def _process_admin_requests(self) -> int:
1214	        processed = 0
1215	        while True:
1216	            try:
1217	                action, payload, response_queue = self._admin_queue.get_nowait()
1218	            except _queue_mod.Empty:
1219	                break
1220	            try:
1221	                response = self._run_admin_action(action, payload)
1222	            except Exception as exc:
1223	                logger.exception("OmniScheduler admin operation failed: %s", action)
1224	                response = {
1225	                    "success": False,
1226	                    "message": str(exc),
1227	                    "error": str(exc),
1228	                }
1229	            response_queue.put(response)
1230	            processed += 1
1231	        return processed
1232	
1233	    def _run_admin_action(
1234	        self, action: str, payload: dict[str, Any] | None = None
1235	    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
1236	        payload = dict(payload or {})
1237	        if action == ADMIN_MODEL_INFO:
1238	            return self._admin_model_info()
1239	        if action == ADMIN_PAUSE_GENERATION:
1240	            return self._admin_pause_generation(payload)
1241	        if action == ADMIN_CONTINUE_GENERATION:
1242	            return self._admin_continue_generation(payload)
1243	        if action == ADMIN_UPDATE_WEIGHTS_FROM_DISK:
1244	            return self._admin_update_weights_from_disk(payload)
1245	        if action == ADMIN_UPDATE_WEIGHTS_FROM_TENSOR:
1246	            return self._admin_update_weights_from_tensor(payload)
1247	        if action == ADMIN_UPDATE_WEIGHTS_FROM_DISTRIBUTED:
1248	            return self._admin_update_weights_from_distributed(payload)
1249	        if action == ADMIN_INIT_WEIGHTS_UPDATE_GROUP:
1250	            return self._admin_init_weights_update_group(payload)
1251	        if action == ADMIN_DESTROY_WEIGHTS_UPDATE_GROUP:
1252	            return self._admin_destroy_weights_update_group(payload)
1253	        if action == ADMIN_WEIGHTS_CHECKER:
1254	            return self._admin_weights_checker(payload)
1255	        return {
1256	            "success": True,
1257	            "message": f"unsupported admin action: {action}",
1258	            "data": {"skipped": True, "unsupported": True},
1259	        }

1264	            info.update(self.model_worker.model_info())
1265	        with self._request_admission_lock:

1278	                "request_build_backlog": request_build_backlog,
1279	                "request_build_max_pending_observed": (
1280	                    self._request_build_max_pending_observed
1281	                ),
1282	                "running_batch_size": len(
1283	                    getattr(self.running_batch, "reqs", []) or []
1284	                ),
1285	                "model_path": self.server_args.model_path,
1286	                "load_format": self.server_args.load_format,
1287	                "weight_version": self.server_args.weight_version,
1288	            }
1289	        )
1290	        return {"success": True, "message": "ok", "data": info}

1292	    def _admin_pause_generation(self, payload: dict[str, Any]) -> dict[str, Any]:
1293	        mode = str(payload.get("mode") or "abort")
1294	        if mode not in {"abort", "retract", "in_place"}:
1295	            return {
1296	                "success": False,
1297	                "message": f"invalid pause mode: {mode}",
1298	                "error": f"invalid pause mode: {mode}",
1299	            }
1300	
1301	        with self._admin_lock:
1302	            self._engine_paused = True
1303	            self._last_pause_mode = mode
1304	            self._resolve_pending_async()
1305	            self._resolve_pending_overlap_results()
1306	            num_paused = 0
1307	            if mode == "abort":
1308	                num_paused = self._abort_all_requests()
1309	            elif mode == "retract":
1310	                num_paused = self._retract_running_requests()
1311	        return {
1312	            "success": True,
1313	            "message": "generation paused",
1314	            "data": {
1315	                "mode": mode,
1316	                "num_paused_requests": num_paused,
1317	                "engine_paused": self._engine_paused,
1318	            },
1319	        }

1324	                self._empty_torch_cache()
1325	            self._engine_paused = False
1326	            self._last_pause_mode = None
1327	        return {
1328	            "success": True,
1329	            "message": "generation continued",
1330	            "data": {"engine_paused": self._engine_paused},
1331	        }

1352	    def _run_weight_update_with_lifecycle(
1353	        self,
1354	        payload: dict[str, Any],
1355	        update_fn,
1356	        result_data: dict[str, Any],
1357	        *,
1358	        keep_pause_on_failure: bool = False,
1359	    ) -> dict[str, Any]:
1360	        keep_pause = bool(payload.get("keep_pause", False))
1361	        keep_engine_paused = keep_pause
1362	        with self._admin_lock:
1363	            previous_pause_state = self._engine_paused
1364	            self._engine_paused = True
1365	            try:
1366	                self._resolve_pending_async()
1367	                self._resolve_pending_overlap_results()
1368	                num_paused = 0
1369	                abort_all_requests = bool(payload.get("abort_all_requests", False))
1370	                if abort_all_requests:
1371	                    num_paused = self._abort_all_requests()
1372	                else:
1373	                    active_request_ids = self._active_request_ids()
1374	                    if active_request_ids and not self._can_update_active_requests(
1375	                        previous_pause_state
1376	                    ):
1377	                        if not keep_pause:
1378	                            self._engine_paused = previous_pause_state
1379	                        return {
1380	                            "success": False,
1381	                            "message": (
1382	                                "active requests are present; set "
1383	                                "abort_all_requests=true or pause_generation with "
1384	                                "mode=retract before updating weights"
1385	                            ),
1386	                            "error": "active requests present during weight update",
1387	                            "data": {
1388	                                "active_request_count": len(active_request_ids),
1389	                                "active_request_ids": active_request_ids[:16],
1390	                                "abort_all_requests": abort_all_requests,
1391	                                "pause_mode": getattr(self, "_last_pause_mode", None),
1392	                                "engine_paused": self._engine_paused,
1393	                            },
1394	                        }
1395	
1396	                try:
1397	                    success, message = update_fn(payload)
1398	                except Exception:
1399	                    if keep_pause_on_failure:
1400	                        keep_engine_paused = True
1401	                    raise
1402	                flush_success: bool | None = None
1403	                if success and bool(payload.get("flush_cache", True)):
1404	                    flush_success = self._flush_cache_after_update()
1405	                    success = success and bool(flush_success)
1406	                    if not flush_success:
1407	                        message = f"{message}; cache flush failed"
1408	
1409	                if keep_pause_on_failure and not success:
1410	                    keep_engine_paused = True
1411	                if bool(payload.get("torch_empty_cache", False)):
1412	                    self._empty_torch_cache()
1413	            finally:
1414	                if keep_engine_paused:
1415	                    self._engine_paused = True
1416	                else:
1417	                    self._engine_paused = previous_pause_state
1418	
1419	        data = {
1420	            "num_paused_requests": num_paused,
1421	            "flush_cache": payload.get("flush_cache", True),
1422	            "flush_success": flush_success,
1423	            "keep_pause": keep_pause,
1424	            "engine_paused": self._engine_paused,
1425	        }
1426	        data.update(result_data)
1427	        return {
1428	            "success": bool(success),
1429	            "message": str(message),
1430	            "data": data,
1431	            "error": None if success else str(message),
1432	        }
1433

1473	    def _admin_init_weights_update_group(
1474	        self, payload: dict[str, Any]
1475	    ) -> dict[str, Any]:

1485	        # init_weights_update_group exposes no timeout, so a missing trainer

1532	    def _abort_all_requests(self) -> int:
1533	        request_ids = self._active_request_ids()
1534	        for request_id in request_ids:
1535	            self.abort(request_id, defer_running_cleanup=False)
1536	        return len(request_ids)
1537	
1538	    def _active_request_ids(self) -> list[str]:
1539	        request_ids: set[str] = set()
1540	        with self._request_admission_lock:
1541	            if self._pending_request_builds:
1542	                request_ids.update(self._pending_request_builds.keys())
1543	            if self._backlogged_request_build_payloads:
1544	                request_ids.update(
1545	                    payload.request_id
1546	                    for payload in self._backlogged_request_build_payloads
1547	                    if payload.request_id not in self._aborted_request_ids
1548	                )
1549	            for req in self.waiting_queue:
1550	                rid = getattr(req, "rid", None)
1551	                if rid is not None:
1552	                    request_ids.add(rid)
1553	        for batch in (
1554	            self.running_batch,
1555	            self.cur_batch,
1556	            self.last_batch,
1557	            self._async_pending_batch(),
1558	        ):
1559	            if batch is None:
1560	                continue
1561	            for req in batch.reqs:
1562	                if req.rid is not None and not req.finished():
1563	                    request_ids.add(req.rid)
1564	        return sorted(request_ids)
1565	
1566	    def _can_update_active_requests(
1567	        self, previously_paused: bool | None = None
1568	    ) -> bool:
1569	        engine_paused = (
1570	            self._engine_paused if previously_paused is None else previously_paused
1571	        )
1572	        return bool(
1573	            engine_paused and getattr(self, "_last_pause_mode", None) == "retract"
1574	        )
1575	
1576	    def _retract_running_requests(self) -> int:
1577	        batch = self.running_batch
1578	        if batch is None or batch.is_empty():
1579	            return 0
1580	        batch.filter_batch(v1_spec_info_filtered=True)
1581	        if len(batch.reqs) == 0:
1582	            return 0
1583	        retracted_reqs = batch.retract_all(self.server_args)
1584	        add_to_queue = getattr(self, "_add_request_to_queue", None)
1585	        for req in retracted_reqs:
1586	            if callable(add_to_queue):
1587	                add_to_queue(req)
1588	            else:
1589	                self.waiting_queue.append(req)
1590	        batch.batch_is_full = False
1591	        self.chunked_req = None
1592	        return len(retracted_reqs)
1593	
1594	    def _flush_cache_after_update(self) -> bool:
1595	        try:
1596	            return bool(self.flush_cache())
1597	        except Exception:
1598	            logger.exception("flush_cache after weight update failed")
1599	            return False

1601	    def _resolve_pending_overlap_results(self) -> None:
1602	        result_queue = getattr(self, "result_queue", None)
1603	        if result_queue is None:
1604	            return
1605	        while result_queue:
1606	            batch, result = result_queue.popleft()
1607	            self.process_batch_result(batch, result)
1608	
1609	    @staticmethod
1610	    def _empty_torch_cache() -> None:
1611	        if not torch.cuda.is_available():
1612	            return
1613	        torch.cuda.empty_cache()
1614	
1615	    def _mark_running_request_aborted(self, request_id: str) -> bool:
1616	        marked = False
1617	        seen: set[int] = set()
1618	        for batch in (
1619	            self.running_batch,
1620	            self.cur_batch,
1621	            self.last_batch,
1622	            self._async_pending_batch(),
1623	        ):
1624	            if batch is None or id(batch) in seen:
1625	                continue
1626	            seen.add(id(batch))
1627	            for req in batch.reqs:
1628	                if req.rid != request_id or req.finished():
1629	                    continue
1630	                req.to_finish = FINISH_ABORT()
1631	                marked = True
1632	        return marked
1633	
1634	    def _release_immediate_request_resources(self, request_id: str) -> None:
1635	        seen: set[int] = set()
1636	        for batch in (
1637	            self.running_batch,
1638	            self.cur_batch,
1639	            self.last_batch,
1640	            self._async_pending_batch(),
1641	        ):
1642	            if batch is None:
1643	                continue
1644	            for req in batch.reqs:
1645	                if req.rid != request_id or id(req) in seen:
1646	                    continue
1647	                seen.add(id(req))
1648	                self._release_request_kv_cache(req)
1649	
1650	    def _release_request_kv_cache(self, req: Any) -> None:
1651	        if req.req_pool_idx is None and req.mamba_pool_idx is None:
1652	            return
1653	        release_kv_cache(req, self.tree_cache)

1655	    def _event_loop_normal(self) -> None:
1656	        # Note (Chenyang): yield the GIL when idle so co-located non-AR stages
1657	        # (encoders, preprocessor) running in sibling threads aren't starved
1658	        # of Python execution. Without this, in single-process mode the busy
1659	        # AR scheduler loop pins the GIL and the audio_encoder forward pass
1660	        # (which is mostly Python-side dispatch into many small CUDA kernels)
1661	        # slows ~600x, dropping audio QPS from >10 to <0.5.
1662	        while self._running:
1663	            self._process_admin_requests()
1664	            recv_reqs = self.recv_requests()
1665	            recv_reqs.extend(self._take_deferred_request_payloads())
1666	            self.process_input_requests(recv_reqs)
1667	            if self._engine_paused:
1668	                self._process_admin_requests()
1669	                time.sleep(0.001)
1670	                continue
1671	
1672	            batch = self.get_next_batch_to_run()
1673	            self.cur_batch = batch
1674	
1675	            if batch:
1676	                result = self.run_batch(batch)
1677	                if result is not _FAILED_BATCH_RESULT:
1678	                    self.process_batch_result(batch, result)
1679	            else:
1680	                self.self_check_during_idle()
1681	                time.sleep(0.001)
1682	
1683	            self.last_batch = batch
1684	            if envs.SGLANG_ENABLE_STRICT_MEM_CHECK_DURING_BUSY.get():
1685	                self.self_check_during_busy()
1686	
1687	    def _event_loop_overlap(self) -> None:
1688	        self.result_queue = deque()
1689	
1690	        def pop_and_process():
1691	            tmp_batch, tmp_result = self.result_queue.popleft()
1692	            self.process_batch_result(tmp_batch, tmp_result)
1693	
1694	        while self._running:
1695	            self._process_admin_requests()
1696	            recv_reqs = self.recv_requests()
1697	            recv_reqs.extend(self._take_deferred_request_payloads())
1698	            self.process_input_requests(recv_reqs)
1699	            if self._engine_paused:
1700	                self._process_admin_requests()
1701	                time.sleep(0.001)
1702	                continue
1703	
1704	            batch = self.get_next_batch_to_run()
1705	            self.cur_batch = batch
1706	            disable_overlap_for_batch = self.is_disable_overlap_for_batch(batch)
1707	
1708	            if disable_overlap_for_batch and self.result_queue:
1709	                pop_and_process()
1710	
1711	            if batch:
1712	                batch_result = self.run_batch(batch)
1713	                if batch_result is not _FAILED_BATCH_RESULT:
1714	                    self.result_queue.append((batch.copy(), batch_result))
1715	                else:
1716	                    batch_result = None
1717	            else:
1718	                batch_result = None
1719	
1720	            if self.last_batch:
1721	                if not disable_overlap_for_batch and self.result_queue:
1722	                    pop_and_process()
1723	            elif batch is None:
1724	                self.self_check_during_idle()
1725	                time.sleep(0.001)
1726	
1727	            if self.is_generation:
1728	                self.launch_batch_sample_if_needed(batch_result)
1729	
1730	            self.last_batch = batch
1731	            if envs.SGLANG_ENABLE_STRICT_MEM_CHECK_DURING_BUSY.get():
1732	                self.self_check_during_busy()
1733	
1734	    @staticmethod
1735	    def _batch_is_decode(batch: ScheduleBatch) -> bool:
1736	        mode = batch.forward_mode
1737	        if mode is None:
1738	            return False
1739	        if mode.is_decode():
1740	            return True
1741	        return not bool(mode.is_extend())
1742	
1743	    def _async_pending_batch(self):
1744	        """The in-flight (launched, not yet resolved) decode batch, or None.
1745	
1746	        ``getattr`` with default so abort paths stay safe even for schedulers
1747	        built without going through ``__init__`` (e.g. unit-test fixtures).
1748	        ``_async_pending`` is ``(batch, sched_output, pending_step)`` or None.
1749	        """
1750	        pending = getattr(self, "_async_pending", None)
1751	        return pending[0] if pending is not None else None
1752	
1753	    def _resolve_and_process(self, batch, sched_output, pending_step) -> None:
1754	        """Resolve a launched step and feed it to process_batch_result, after
1755	        dropping requests that already finished in an earlier step.
1756	
1757	        Lookahead overrun: a request that finishes at step S is still present in
1758	        step S+1's (already-launched) batch — its S+1 output is discarded by the
1759	        collect's ``_cg_was_done`` skip, but upstream process_batch_result would
1760	        re-free its KV. So drop reqs that were ALREADY finished in an earlier
1761	        step (and their next_token_ids rows) from this lagged batch.
1762	
1763	        Crucially, snapshot finished-state BEFORE the resolve: a req that
1764	        finishes *during* this step's collect (e.g. an EOC finish, which
1765	        _mark_sampler_finished sets) must be KEPT so process_batch_result emits
1766	        it — only reqs finished in a *prior* step are the overrun to drop.
1767	        """
1768	        # A request retracted at step S is still in step S+1's lagged batch;
1769	        # drop it like a prior-step finish so its KV is not re-freed.
1770	        pre_finished = [
1771	            r.finished() or bool(getattr(r, "is_retracted", False)) for r in batch.reqs
1772	        ]
1773	        # rids finished/retracted in a prior step (overrun): suppress their emit
1774	        skip_rids = {batch.reqs[i].rid for i, was in enumerate(pre_finished) if was}
1775	        result = self._run_batch_resolve(
1776	            batch, sched_output, pending_step, skip_rids=skip_rids
1777	        )
1778	        if result is _FAILED_BATCH_RESULT:
1779	            return
1780	        keep = [i for i, was_finished in enumerate(pre_finished) if not was_finished]
1781	        if len(keep) < len(batch.reqs):
1782	            if result.next_token_ids is not None and keep:
1783	                idx = torch.tensor(keep, device=result.next_token_ids.device)
1784	                result.next_token_ids = result.next_token_ids[idx]
1785	            # Drop overrun reqs from the batch. NOT filter_batch(): batch is a
1786	            # ScheduleBatch.copy() which omits seq_lens (it carries only the
1787	            # fields process_batch_result needs). process_batch_result_decode
1788	            # zips batch.reqs with next_token_ids and uses Req attributes (not
1789	            # positional batch tensors), so trimming reqs in lockstep suffices.
1790	            batch.reqs = [batch.reqs[i] for i in keep]
1791	        if batch.reqs:
1792	            self.process_batch_result(batch, result)
1793	
1794	    def _resolve_pending_async(self) -> None:
1795	        """Resolve + process the in-flight decode step, if any. Used to flush
1796	        before prefill / pause / shutdown so a launched step is never stranded.
1797	        """
1798	        if self._async_pending is None:
1799	            return
1800	        batch, sched_output, pending_step = self._async_pending
1801	        self._async_pending = None
1802	        try:
1803	            self._resolve_and_process(batch, sched_output, pending_step)
1804	        except Exception as exc:
1805	            self._handle_batch_failure(batch, exc)
1806	
1807	    def _free_overrun_step_slots(self, out_cache_loc, drop_indices) -> None:
1808	        """Free the per-step decode KV slot for rows whose request finished or
1809	        retracted in a prior step (the lookahead overrun): ``prepare_for_decode``
1810	        allocated it but ``cache_finished_req`` truncates below it, so it leaks.
1811	
1812	        Only under RadixCache + page_size=1; ChunkCache/paged already free the slot
1813	        with the request, so compensating here would double-free — hence the gate.
1814	        """
1815	        if not drop_indices:
1816	            return
1817	        if self.page_size != 1 or self.server_args.disable_radix_cache:
1818	            return
1819	        if out_cache_loc is None:
1820	            logger.warning("overrun step-slot free skipped: out_cache_loc is None")
1821	            return
1822	        assert max(drop_indices) < out_cache_loc.numel(), (
1823	            f"overrun drop index {max(drop_indices)} out of range "
1824	            f"({out_cache_loc.numel()} step slots)"
1825	        )
1826	        idx = torch.tensor(drop_indices, dtype=torch.long, device=out_cache_loc.device)
1827	        self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator.free(out_cache_loc[idx])
1828	
1829	    def _drop_stale_overrun(self, batch):
1830	        """Drop reqs finished OR retracted by the just-completed drain from the
1831	        stale fast-path batch, so run_batch does not forward/finalize them again
1832	        (double-free of already-freed KV). Returns the filtered batch, or None if
1833	        it empties. Mirrors the finished/is_retracted pre-drop in
1834	        _resolve_and_process; the fast path previously dropped only finished.
1835	        """
1836	        if batch is None or not batch.reqs:
1837	            return batch
1838	        drop = [
1839	            r.finished() or bool(getattr(r, "is_retracted", False)) for r in batch.reqs
1840	        ]
1841	        if not any(drop):
1842	            return batch
1843	        keep = [i for i, d in enumerate(drop) if not d]
1844	        out_cache_loc = batch.out_cache_loc
1845	        self._free_overrun_step_slots(
1846	            out_cache_loc, [i for i, d in enumerate(drop) if d]
1847	        )
1848	        batch.filter_batch(keep_indices=keep)
1849	        if out_cache_loc is not None:
1850	            batch.out_cache_loc = out_cache_loc[keep]
1851	        return batch if batch.reqs else None
1852	
1853	    def _event_loop_async_decode(self) -> None:
1854	        """One-step-lookahead decode loop (single stream + CUDA event).
1855	
1856	        Each iteration LAUNCHES the current decode step (GPU forward + on-GPU
1857	        sample, then ``post_decode_launch`` publishes the resolve payload, no GPU
1858	        wait) and THEN RESOLVES the previous step's host-side collect, so the
1859	        resolve host work overlaps the current step's GPU forward (launch-first,
1860	        D1 in design.md section 1.3). Prefill / empty batches flush any in-flight
1861	        decode first and run synchronously (the in-flight step is never stranded).
1862	        """
1863	        while self._running:
1864	            self._process_admin_requests()
1865	            recv_reqs = self.recv_requests()
1866	            recv_reqs.extend(self._take_deferred_request_payloads())
1867	            self.process_input_requests(recv_reqs)
1868	            if self._engine_paused:
1869	                self._process_admin_requests()
1870	                self._resolve_pending_async()
1871	                time.sleep(0.001)
1872	                continue
1873	
1874	            if (
1875	                self._async_pending is not None
1876	                and self.is_mixed_chunk
1877	                and (
1878	                    self.chunked_req is not None
1879	                    or (self.waiting_queue and not self.running_batch.batch_is_full)
1880	                )
1881	            ):
1882	                self._resolve_pending_async()
1883	
1884	            batch = self.get_next_batch_to_run()
1885	            self.cur_batch = batch
1886	
1887	            # Route through sync when the runner's collect has a sync-only
1888	            # fallback (default True for runners not overriding lookahead_eligible).
1889	            runner = getattr(self, "_model_runner", None)
1890	            use_lookahead = (
1891	                batch is not None
1892	                and len(batch.reqs) >= self.async_decode_min_batch_size
1893	                and self._batch_is_decode(batch)
1894	                and (runner is None or runner.lookahead_eligible(batch))
1895	            )
1896	
1897	            if use_lookahead:
1898	                try:
1899	                    sched_output, pending_step = self._run_batch_launch(batch)
1900	                except Exception as exc:
1901	                    self._handle_batch_failure(batch, exc)
1902	                else:
1903	                    prev_pending = self._async_pending
1904	                    self._async_pending = (batch.copy(), sched_output, pending_step)
1905	                    if prev_pending is not None:
1906	                        pb, ps, pstep = prev_pending
1907	                        try:
1908	                            self._resolve_and_process(pb, ps, pstep)
1909	                        except Exception as exc:
1910	                            self._handle_batch_failure(pb, exc)
1911	            else:
1912	                # Fast path (low-concurrency decode below the threshold) +
1913	                # prefill + empty all land here: flush any in-flight lookahead
1914	                # step first (preserve ordering — this is also the bs>=2 -> bs=1
1915	                # drain transition), then run this batch synchronously. Bypassing
1916	                # the lookahead at bs=1 avoids its fixed per-step overhead, which
1917	                # at low concurrency has no overlap payoff (the bs=1 regression).
1918	                # Skip the drain call entirely in the common no-pending case (the
1919	                # bs=1 steady state) — _resolve_pending_async would just no-op.
1920	                if self._async_pending is not None:
1921	                    self._resolve_pending_async()
1922	                    # Stale-batch overrun: `batch` was built (get_next_batch_to_run,
1923	                    # top of loop) BEFORE this drain, which can finish OR retract reqs
1924	                    # still present in it. Drop them before run_batch so they are not
1925	                    # forwarded/finalized a second time (double-free of already-freed
1926	                    # KV). Fast-path analogue of the _resolve_and_process drop.
1927	                    batch = self._drop_stale_overrun(batch)
1928	                    self.cur_batch = batch
1929	                if batch:
1930	                    result = self.run_batch(batch)
1931	                    if result is not _FAILED_BATCH_RESULT:
1932	                        self.process_batch_result(batch, result)
1933	                else:
1934	                    self.self_check_during_idle()
1935	                    time.sleep(0.001)
1936	
1937	            self.last_batch = batch
1938	            if envs.SGLANG_ENABLE_STRICT_MEM_CHECK_DURING_BUSY.get():
1939	                self.self_check_during_busy()
1940	
1941	    def _drain_inbox_for_request(self, request_id: str) -> None:
1942	        retained: list[IncomingMessage] = []
1943	        while True:
1944	            try:
1945	                msg = self.inbox.get_nowait()
1946	            except _queue_mod.Empty:
1947	                break
1948	            if msg.request_id != request_id:
1949	                retained.append(msg)
1950	        for msg in retained:
1951	            self.inbox.put(msg)
1952	
1953	    def _find_request_data(self, request_id: str) -> Any | None:
1954	        # Scan all batches a live req can sit in during prefill→decode handoff.
1955	        for batch in (self.running_batch, self.cur_batch, self.last_batch):
1956	            if batch is None:
1957	                continue
1958	            for req in batch.reqs:
1959	                if req.rid == request_id:
1960	                    return req._omni_data
1961	        for req in self.waiting_queue:
1962	            if req.rid == request_id:
1963	                return req._omni_data
1964	        return None
1965	
1966	    @staticmethod
1967	    def _append_stream_chunk_default(req_data: Any, chunk: Any) -> None:
1968	        stream_chunks = getattr(req_data, "stream_chunks", None)
1969	        if stream_chunks is None:
1970	            stream_chunks = deque()
1971	            req_data.stream_chunks = stream_chunks
1972	        stream_chunks.append(chunk)
1973	
1974	    def _append_stream_chunk(self, req_data: Any, chunk: Any) -> None:
1975	        if self._stream_chunk_handler is None:
1976	            self._append_stream_chunk_default(req_data, chunk)
1977	            return
1978	        self._stream_chunk_handler(req_data, chunk)
1979	
1980	    def _mark_stream_done(self, req_data: Any) -> None:
1981	        if self._stream_done_handler is None:
1982	            req_data.stream_done = True
1983	            return
1984	        self._stream_done_handler(req_data)
1985	
1986	
1987	def _remove_from_batch(batch: Any, request_id: str) -> None:
1988	    if batch is None:
1989	        return
1990	    batch.reqs = [req for req in batch.reqs if req.rid != request_id]
1991	    if not batch.reqs:
1992	        batch.batch_is_full = False
1993

# SPDX-License-Identifier: Apache-2.0
"""OmniScheduler — stage-facing AR scheduler using composition.

Uses SGLang's batch selection and result processing logic via **unbound
method calls** on the upstream ``Scheduler`` class.  No inheritance.

When an upstream method (e.g. ``get_next_batch_to_run``) internally calls
``self.get_new_batch_prefill()``, Python finds it through
``OmniScheduler.__getattr__`` → looks it up on the upstream class → binds
it to this instance.  This gives us the full scheduling MRO without
inheriting from ``SGLangScheduler``.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import logging
import queue as _queue_mod
import threading
import time
import types
from collections import deque
from concurrent.futures import Future, ThreadPoolExecutor
from typing import Any, Callable

import torch
from sglang.srt.environ import envs
from sglang.srt.layers.dp_attention import compute_dp_attention_world_info
from sglang.srt.managers.schedule_batch import FINISH_ABORT, ScheduleBatch
from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler import Scheduler as _Upstream
from sglang.srt.managers.scheduler import validate_input_length
from sglang.srt.mem_cache.common import release_kv_cache
from sglang.srt.utils import broadcast_pyobj

from sglang_omni.profiler.event_recorder import emit as _emit_event
from sglang_omni.profiler.event_recorder import get_active_stage as _get_active_stage
from sglang_omni.proto.admin import (
    ADMIN_CONTINUE_GENERATION,
    ADMIN_DESTROY_WEIGHTS_UPDATE_GROUP,
    ADMIN_INIT_WEIGHTS_UPDATE_GROUP,
    ADMIN_MODEL_INFO,
    ADMIN_PAUSE_GENERATION,
    ADMIN_UPDATE_WEIGHTS_FROM_DISK,
    ADMIN_UPDATE_WEIGHTS_FROM_DISTRIBUTED,
    ADMIN_UPDATE_WEIGHTS_FROM_TENSOR,
    ADMIN_WEIGHTS_CHECKER,
)
from sglang_omni.scheduling.messages import IncomingMessage, OutgoingMessage

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

_FAILED_BATCH_RESULT = object()

_ABORTED_REQUEST_ID_LIMIT = 10000
_ABORTED_REQUEST_ID_RETAINED = 5000


class _NoOpSender:
    """Stub for send_to_detokenizer — stream_output handles emission."""

    def send_output(self, *args, **kwargs):
        pass


class _NoOpGrammarManager:
    """Stub — OmniScheduler never uses constrained decoding."""

    grammar_queue: list = []

    def has_waiting_grammars(self) -> bool:
        return False

    def get_ready_grammar_requests(self) -> list:
        return []

    def abort_requests(self, recv_req) -> None:
        pass

    def clear(self) -> None:
        pass

    def __len__(self) -> int:
        return 0


class OmniScheduler:
    """Stage-facing scheduler for AR stages.

    Public contract (used by Stage):
        ``inbox``, ``outbox``, ``start()``, ``stop()``, ``abort(request_id)``

    Composition strategy:
        SGLang scheduling methods (``get_next_batch_to_run``,
        ``process_batch_result``, …) are looked up on the upstream
        ``Scheduler`` *class* via ``__getattr__`` and called with this
        instance as ``self``.  Methods we override (``recv_requests``,
        ``process_input_requests``, ``run_batch``, ``send_to_tokenizer``)
        are defined directly on this class and take precedence.
    """

    def __init__(
        self,
        tp_worker: Any,
        tree_cache: Any,
        req_to_token_pool: Any,
        token_to_kv_pool_allocator: Any,
        server_args: Any,
        model_config: Any,
        *,
        prefill_manager: Any = None,
        decode_manager: Any = None,
        model_runner: Any = None,
        request_builder: Callable | None = None,
        result_adapter: Callable | None = None,
        stream_output_builder: Callable | None = None,
        stream_chunk_handler: Callable | None = None,
        stream_done_handler: Callable | None = None,
        abort_callback: Callable[[str], None] | None = None,
        enable_overlap: bool = False,
        enable_async_decode: bool = False,
        async_decode_min_batch_size: int = 2,
        prefill_coalesce_requests: int = 0,
        prefill_coalesce_wait_ms: float = 60.0,
        request_build_max_workers: int = 1,
        request_build_max_pending: int | None = None,
        shutdown_callback: Callable[[], None] | None = None,
    ):
        self.inbox: _queue_mod.Queue[IncomingMessage] = _queue_mod.Queue()
        self.outbox: _queue_mod.Queue[OutgoingMessage] = _queue_mod.Queue()
        self.requires_tp_work_fanout: bool = False

        # --- Request builder: StagePayload → SGLangARRequestData ----------
        self._request_builder = request_builder
        self._result_adapter = result_adapter
        self._model_runner = model_runner
        self._stream_output_builder = stream_output_builder
        self._stream_chunk_handler = stream_chunk_handler
        self._stream_done_handler = stream_done_handler
        self._abort_callback = abort_callback
        self._shutdown_callback = shutdown_callback
        self._shutdown_lock = threading.Lock()
        self._request_admission_lock = threading.RLock()
        self.request_build_max_workers = max(1, int(request_build_max_workers))
        if self.request_build_max_workers > 1 and int(server_args.tp_size) > 1:
            logger.warning(
                "OmniScheduler request-build workers are disabled for "
                f"tp_size={server_args.tp_size} to preserve identical request "
                "admission order on every TP rank"
            )
            self.request_build_max_workers = 1
        if self.request_build_max_workers > 1:
            max_pending = (
                self.request_build_max_workers
                if request_build_max_pending is None
                else int(request_build_max_pending)
            )
            self.request_build_max_pending = max(1, max_pending)
            self._request_build_backlog_limit = max(
                self.request_build_max_pending,
                int(server_args.max_queued_requests or 0),
            )
            self._request_build_executor: ThreadPoolExecutor | None = (
                ThreadPoolExecutor(
                    max_workers=self.request_build_max_workers,
                    thread_name_prefix="omni-request-build",
                )
            )
        else:
            self.request_build_max_pending = 0
            self._request_build_backlog_limit = 0
            self._request_build_executor = None
        self._pending_request_builds: dict[str, tuple[Any, bool, Future]] = {}
        self._backlogged_request_build_payloads: deque[Any] = deque()
        self._request_build_max_pending_observed = 0

        # --- Core scheduling state (read/written by upstream methods) -----
        self.server_args = server_args
        self.model_config = model_config
        self.gpu_id = tp_worker.gpu_id
        self.tp_rank = getattr(tp_worker, "tp_rank", 0)
        self.tp_size = server_args.tp_size
        self.pp_rank = 0
        self.pp_size = server_args.pp_size
        self.dp_rank = None
        self.dp_size = 1
        self.moe_ep_rank = 0
        self.moe_ep_size = 1
        self.page_size = server_args.page_size
        self.enable_overlap = enable_overlap
        # One-step-lookahead async decode (single stream + CUDA event). Only
        # safe for model runners that implement post_decode_launch/resolve.
        self.enable_async_decode = enable_async_decode
        # Below this decode batch size the lookahead is bypassed for a plain
        # synchronous step: at low concurrency the per-step collect is too small
        # to overlap, so the lookahead's fixed overhead is a net loss (the bs=1
        # regression — see benchmark_results.md / stall_analysis.md). Default 2
        # = only bs=1 takes the fast path.
        self.async_decode_min_batch_size = int(async_decode_min_batch_size)
        if model_runner is not None:
            model_runner._async_enabled = enable_async_decode

        # Note: (maydomine) coalescing gate: hold prefill until K requests wait
        # or the oldest has waited T ms; 0 disables.
        if int(prefill_coalesce_requests) > 1 and int(server_args.tp_size) > 1:
            logger.warning(
                "Prefill admission coalescing is disabled for "
                f"tp_size={server_args.tp_size}: the wait deadline reads each "
                "rank's local clock, so ranks could disagree on expiry and "
                "break lockstep scheduling"
            )
            prefill_coalesce_requests = 0
        self.prefill_coalesce_requests = int(prefill_coalesce_requests)
        self.prefill_coalesce_wait_s = float(prefill_coalesce_wait_ms) / 1e3

        # Token / memory info (upstream reads from tp_worker.get_worker_info)
        mr = tp_worker.model_runner
        self.max_total_num_tokens = mr.max_total_num_tokens
        self.max_prefill_tokens = server_args.max_prefill_tokens
        self.max_running_requests = server_args.max_running_requests
        self.max_queued_requests = server_args.max_queued_requests
        self.max_req_len = min(
            server_args.context_length - 1,
            self.max_total_num_tokens - 1,
        )
        self.max_req_input_len = self.max_req_len - 1
        self.random_seed = tp_worker.random_seed
        self.device = tp_worker.device

        # Global server_args field upstream sets in its __init__
        from sglang.srt.server_args import get_global_server_args

        gsa = get_global_server_args()
        if gsa is not None and gsa.pp_max_micro_batch_size is None:
            gsa.pp_max_micro_batch_size = max(
                self.max_running_requests // self.pp_size,
                1,
            )

        # Workers
        self.tp_worker = tp_worker
        self.model_worker = tp_worker

        # Cache / memory management
        self.tree_cache = tree_cache
        self.req_to_token_pool = req_to_token_pool
        self.token_to_kv_pool_allocator = token_to_kv_pool_allocator
        self.prefill_manager = prefill_manager
        self.decode_manager = decode_manager

        # Batch state
        self.waiting_queue: list = []
        self.running_batch = ScheduleBatch(reqs=[], batch_is_full=False)
        self.cur_batch = None
        self.last_batch = None
        # Async decode (one-step lookahead): the launched-but-not-resolved
        # decode batch, or None. Tracked here (not just a loop local) so abort
        # can reach the in-flight step. See _event_loop_async_decode.
        self._async_pending = None
        self.forward_ct = 0
        self.return_health_check_ct = 0
        self.num_retracted_reqs = 0
        self.num_paused_reqs = 0
        self.sessions: dict = {}
        self.forward_sleep_time = None
        self._engine_paused = False
        self._admin_lock = threading.Lock()
        self._admin_queue = _queue_mod.Queue()
        self._scheduler_thread_id: int | None = None
        self._last_pause_mode: str | None = None

        # Chunked prefill
        self.chunked_prefill_size = server_args.chunked_prefill_size
        if self.chunked_prefill_size <= 0:
            self.chunked_prefill_size = None
        self.chunked_req = None
        self.is_mixed_chunk = (
            self.chunked_prefill_size is not None and server_args.enable_mixed_chunk
        )
        self.enable_dynamic_chunking = False

        # Schedule policy
        from sglang.srt.managers.schedule_policy import SchedulePolicy

        self.schedule_policy = server_args.schedule_policy
        self.policy = SchedulePolicy(
            self.schedule_policy,
            self.tree_cache,
            server_args.enable_hierarchical_cache,
            server_args.enable_priority_scheduling,
            server_args.schedule_low_priority_values_first,
        )
        self.enable_priority_scheduling = server_args.enable_priority_scheduling
        self.try_preemption = server_args.enable_priority_scheduling
        self.priority_scheduling_preemption_threshold = (
            server_args.priority_scheduling_preemption_threshold
        )
        self.schedule_low_priority_values_first = (
            server_args.schedule_low_priority_values_first
        )
        self.init_new_token_ratio = min(
            envs.SGLANG_INIT_NEW_TOKEN_RATIO.get()
            * server_args.schedule_conservativeness,
            1.0,
        )
        self.min_new_token_ratio = min(
            self.init_new_token_ratio * envs.SGLANG_MIN_NEW_TOKEN_RATIO_FACTOR.get(),
            1.0,
        )
        self.new_token_ratio_decay = (
            self.init_new_token_ratio - self.min_new_token_ratio
        ) / envs.SGLANG_NEW_TOKEN_RATIO_DECAY_STEPS.get()
        self.new_token_ratio = self.init_new_token_ratio
        self.prefill_delayer = None

        # Feature flags (all disabled)
        self.enable_lora = False
        self.enable_pdmux = False
        self.enable_metrics = server_args.enable_metrics
        self.enable_trace = False
        self.enable_hierarchical_cache = False
        self.enable_hicache_storage = False
        self.enable_kv_cache_events = False
        self.is_generation = True
        self.skip_tokenizer_init = True
        self.stream_interval = 1
        self.max_recv_per_poll = 64
        self.enable_lora_overlap_loading = False
        self.enable_metrics_for_all_schedulers = (
            server_args.enable_metrics_for_all_schedulers
        )
